address: Sri Lanna National Park, Mae Taeng District

description.en:
## Overview

Bua Thong Sticky Waterfalls, located about 60 kilometers north of Chiang Mai in Sri Lanna National Park, offer one of the most unusual natural experiences in all of Thailand. Unlike conventional waterfalls where water cascades over slippery rocks, the limestone formations here have a rough, porous texture that provides remarkable grip underfoot. Visitors can literally walk and climb up the cascading water without slipping, earning the falls their famous nickname.

## History

The waterfalls are part of Sri Lanna National Park, established to protect the forests and watersheds of the Mae Taeng and Mae Ngat river basins. The unique sticky texture is caused by calcium carbonate deposits in the limestone, which create a rough surface as mineral-rich water flows over the rock. Over thousands of years, this geological process has built up layered travertine formations that feel almost like wet sandpaper, making them safe to climb even while water rushes over them.

## What to See

The falls consist of three main tiers, each with pools perfect for cooling off. The lowest tier is the easiest to climb and most popular with families. The middle and upper tiers offer a more adventurous scramble through lush jungle surroundings. Ropes are strung along steeper sections for extra support, though most visitors find the natural grip sufficient. The surrounding forest is home to butterflies, tropical birds, and towering hardwood trees that provide welcome shade.

## Practical Info

The waterfalls are free to enter and open daily from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Getting here requires your own transport or a tour, as there is no public bus service. The drive from Chiang Mai city takes roughly 1 to 1.5 hours. Wear water shoes or sandals with good grip, and bring a change of clothes since you will get wet. There are basic changing facilities, toilets, and a small food stall near the parking area. Weekday mornings are the quietest time to visit. The dry season from November to April offers the best conditions, with clear water and comfortable temperatures.
description.nl: Unieke kalkstenen watervallen die je kunt beklimmen zonder uit te glijden

best_time_to_visit: November to April (dry season), weekday mornings for fewer crowds
tips: Wear water shoes or sandals with good grip as you will climb through running water
Bring a change of clothes and a waterproof bag for your phone
Arrive early on weekdays to have the falls nearly to yourself
